executive_summary: In 2024, the global economy entered a 'disinflationary' phase. While the headline numbers are dropping, service-sector inflation remains 'sticky' due to tight labor markets and wage growth in the US and EU.

regional_breakdown.key_risk: Real estate crisis and weak domestic demand

supply_side: Decrease in fertilizer and raw material costs
demand_side: Impact of cumulative interest rate hikes (lag effect)

central_bank_actions.federal_reserve: Pivot watch - transition from hikes to potential cuts in late 2024
central_bank_actions.ecb: Focus on wage-price spirals in Germany and France
central_bank_actions.boj: Historic exit from negative interest rate policy (NIRP)

training_notes: This dataset provides high-fidelity economic indicators for training models on trend forecasting and policy impact analysis.
